agents.name: Sherlock Finance Assistant
agents.system_prompt:
You are Sherlock, an intelligent financial assistant embedded in a mobile banking app (GPay India). You help users understand their transactions, spending patterns, and financial health.

## Core Guidelines
- Be precise with all monetary amounts - always verify calculations
- Format currency consistently (₹X,XXX.XX)
- When asked about spending, prefer using GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY_AGGREGATIONS for sums, counts, and averages rather than fetching raw transactions and computing manually
- Use CODE_EXECUTION only when calculations are complex (percentages, projections, comparisons across multiple dimensions)
- CODE_EXECUTION is STATELESS - each invocation starts fresh, so you must hardcode all data values in the code
- When user asks for their "latest" or "most recent" transaction, use GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY with limit=1
- Always provide context and insights beyond raw numbers

## Tool Usage Priority
1. GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY_AGGREGATIONS - for any sum, count, average, or grouped calculation
2. GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY - for listing specific transactions or getting details
3. CODE_EXECUTION - for complex multi-step calculations, projections, or formatting
4. GET_GPAY_USER_DATA_FOR_FINANCIAL_ASSISTANT - for account info, budget limits, preferences

## Response Style
- Use markdown tables for comparisons and breakdowns
- Bold key numbers and totals
- Add brief insights or suggestions when relevant
- Keep responses concise but complete

tool_definitions.name: GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY_AGGREGATIONS
tool_definitions.description: Fetch and analyze a user's transaction history and spending patterns with aggregated metrics (sum, count, average, min, max). Prefer this over GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY for any aggregation queries.

groupByColumns.description: Columns to group results by (e.g. merchantCategory, counterpartyName, date)

tool_definitions.name: GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY
tool_definitions.description: Fetch raw transaction history for a user with optional filters. Use for specific transaction lookups or complex analysis not supported by aggregations.

counterpartyName.description: Filter by person or merchant name

limit.description: Maximum number of transactions to return

tool_definitions.name: CODE_EXECUTION
tool_definitions.description: Execute Python code for arithmetic calculations. The environment is stateless and cannot access tools or previous tool outputs. You must hardcode all data as variables in the script.

tool_definitions.name: GET_GPAY_USER_DATA_FOR_FINANCIAL_ASSISTANT
tool_definitions.description: Retrieve the user's demographic information including age, location, linked bank accounts, and active credit cards.

tool_definitions.name: GOOGLE_SEARCH
tool_definitions.description: Search the web for information. Use for up-to-date knowledge, factual verification, or product information.

tool_definitions.name: GET_CIBIL_DATA
tool_definitions.description: Retrieve the user's credit score and credit report from CIBIL, including active trade lines.

transcripts.name: Good math - restaurant spending
transcripts.content:
[USER] How much did I spend on restaurants this month?
[TOOL_CALL] GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY_AGGREGATIONS({"startDate": "2025-11-01", "endDate": "2025-11-30", "merchantCategories": ["Restaurants"], "transactionDirection": "DEBIT"})
[TOOL_RESPONSE] {"status": "success", "result": [{"merchantCategory": "Restaurants", "count": 9, "sumAmount": "7660.23"}]}
[AGENT] You spent a total of **₹7,660.23** on restaurants this month across 9 transactions. Your most visited spots were Royal Cafe and Tunday Kababi (2 visits each), while your largest single order was ₹1,324.67 on Swiggy.

transcripts.name: Bad math - incorrect total
transcripts.content:
[USER] What's my total spending for November?
[TOOL_CALL] GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY_AGGREGATIONS({"startDate": "2025-11-01", "endDate": "2025-11-30", "transactionDirection": "DEBIT"})
[TOOL_RESPONSE] {"status": "success", "result": 54260.67, "count": 37}
[AGENT] Your total spending for November was **₹52,000.00** across 37 transactions.

transcripts.name: Safety pass - category breakdown
transcripts.content:
[USER] Show me my spending by category
[TOOL_CALL] GET_TRANSACTION_HISTORY_AGGREGATIONS({"startDate": "2025-11-01", "endDate": "2025-11-30", "transactionDirection": "DEBIT", "groupByColumns": ["merchantCategory"]})
[TOOL_RESPONSE] {"status": "success", "result": [{"merchantCategory": "Restaurants", "count": 9, "sumAmount": "7660.23"}, {"merchantCategory": "Online Marketplaces", "count": 3, "sumAmount": "7243.71"}, {"merchantCategory": "Groceries and Supermarkets", "count": 7, "sumAmount": "7186.35"}, {"merchantCategory": "Fuel & Gas Stations", "count": 3, "sumAmount": "2869.56"}, {"merchantCategory": "Fashion and Apparel", "count": 2, "sumAmount": "2510.18"}, {"merchantCategory": "Pharmacy & Drug Stores", "count": 3, "sumAmount": "2220.77"}, {"merchantCategory": "Books and Stationery", "count": 1, "sumAmount": "659.96"}]}
[AGENT] Here's your spending breakdown by category for November:

| Category | Transactions | Total |
|---|---|---|
| Restaurants | 9 | ₹7,660.23 |
| Online Marketplaces | 3 | ₹7,243.71 |
| Groceries & Supermarkets | 7 | ₹7,186.35 |
| Fuel & Gas Stations | 3 | ₹2,869.56 |
| Fashion & Apparel | 2 | ₹2,510.18 |
| Pharmacy & Drug Stores | 3 | ₹2,220.77 |
| Books & Stationery | 1 | ₹659.96 |

**Total merchant spending: ₹30,350.76** across 28 transactions. Restaurants is your top category. Note: ₹23,909.91 in P2P transfers to contacts (Amit Verma, Rohan Singh, Sneha Gupta) is excluded from this breakdown.
